keyPoints: Junk Dimensions consolidate low-cardinality flags and indicators into a single dimension to simplify the fact table.
Role-Playing Dimensions are the same physical dimension table used multiple times in a schema with different foreign keys and aliases (e.g., a Date dimension for both Order Date and Ship Date).
A Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) is a dimension that captures data changes over time, with Type 2 (adding new row) being the most common method for tracking history.
commonMistakes: Creating a separate dimension for every single flag or indicator, leading to a overly complex schema with too many tables.
Treating a role-playing dimension as multiple physical tables instead of a single table joined multiple times.
Using the wrong SCD type (e.g., Type 1 overwrite) when historical tracking is required, causing loss of data history.
